ocr.text: The increasing emphasis on sustainable construction practices across the Asia-Pacific region has led to notable trends in green
building certifications and the adoption of Building Information Modeling (BIM). As the construction industry evolves, the
demand for environmentally responsible materials is reflected in the sales metrics for various building materials. For instance,
wood's Units Sold in the recent reporting period reached 600, with an encouraging trend observed in the adoption of BIM
technologies, which enhance project efficiency and sustainability. Notably, the total value of projects employing BIM reached a
significant milestone, with a reported average of 3.5 certifications per project.
Materials  Revenue($M)  YoYChange(%)  MarketShare(%)  Revenue($M)  YoYChange(%)  MarketShare(%)
Wood  [HIDDEN]  [HIDDEN]  8  620  4  [HIDDEN]  640  3  10
Insulation  400  2  5  410  3  5  420  2  6
The workforce in this sector is also adapting, with a headcount increase of 20%, illustrating the industry's commitment to
meeting growing sustainability demands. Furthermore, the statistics indicate a strong performance in the adoption of innovative
materials, such as steel, where the Units Sold peaked at 800. In terms of project completion timelines, the average percentage of
projects completed within 12 months stands at 60%, showcasing operational efficiencies.

As the sector continues on this trajectory, the data suggests that by 2000, it is projected that 92% of new constructions will
integrate green building practices, underlining a significant shift towards sustainable development in the region.
